prompt_text: Create a weekly meal and fitness plan.
response_text:
**Weekly Meal Plan**

This meal plan is designed to provide approximately 2000 calories per day, with a balance of protein, healthy fats, and complex carbohydrates. Feel free to adjust the portion sizes based on your individual calorie needs.

**Monday**

* Breakfast: Overnight oats with banana and almond milk (400 calories, 30g protein)
* Lunch: Grilled chicken breast with quinoa and mixed vegetables (500 calories, 40g protein)
* Snack: Apple slices with peanut butter (150 calories, 8g protein)
* Dinner: Baked salmon with sweet potato and green beans (500 calories, 35g protein)

**Tuesday**

* Breakfast: Scrambled eggs with whole wheat toast and avocado (350 calories, 20g protein)
* Lunch: Turkey and avocado wrap with mixed greens (550 calories, 30g protein)
* Snack: Greek yogurt with berries and honey (200 calories, 15g protein)
* Dinner: Grilled chicken breast with roasted vegetables and brown rice (500 calories, 40g protein)

**Wednesday**

* Breakfast: Smoothie bowl with banana, spinach, and almond milk (400 calories, 20g protein)
* Lunch: Grilled chicken Caesar salad (500 calories, 40g protein)
* Snack: Carrot sticks with hummus (100 calories, 5g protein)
* Dinner: Slow-cooked beef stew with quinoa and mixed vegetables (550 calories, 35g protein)

**Thursday**

* Breakfast: Avocado toast with scrambled eggs and cherry tomatoes (350 calories, 20g protein)
* Lunch: Chicken and quinoa bowl with roasted vegetables (500 calories, 30g protein)
* Snack: Protein bar (200 calories, 15g protein)
* Dinner: Grilled shrimp with zucchini noodles and cherry tomatoes (400 calories, 25g protein)

**Friday**

* Breakfast: Omelette with whole wheat toast and mixed vegetables (300 calories, 20g protein)
* Lunch: Turkey and cheese sandwich on whole wheat bread with a side salad (550 calories, 30g protein)
* Snack: Rice cakes with almond butter and banana slices (200 calories, 8g protein)
* Dinner: Baked chicken breast with roasted sweet potatoes and green beans (500 calories, 40g protein)

**Saturday**

* Breakfast: Breakfast burrito with scrambled eggs, black beans, and avocado (400 calories, 20g protein)
* Lunch: Grilled chicken breast with mixed greens and whole wheat wrap (500 calories, 40g protein)
* Snack: Apple slices with cheddar cheese (150 calories, 10g protein)
* Dinner: Slow-cooked chicken and vegetable soup with whole wheat bread (500 calories, 30g protein)

**Sunday**

* Breakfast: Breakfast tacos with scrambled eggs, black beans, and avocado (400 calories, 20g protein)
* Lunch: Turkey and cheese wrap with mixed greens and whole wheat wrap (550 calories, 30g protein)
* Snack: Greek yogurt with honey and almonds (200 calories, 15g protein)
* Dinner: Baked salmon with roasted asparagus and quinoa (500 calories, 35g protein)

**Weekly Fitness Plan**

This fitness plan is designed to provide a balance of cardiovascular exercise, strength training, and flexibility exercises. Aim to exercise for at least 150 minutes per week, with at least one day of rest.

**Monday**

* Warm-up: 5-minute dynamic stretching (leg swings, arm circles, hip circles)
* Cardio: 30-minute jog or brisk walk
* Strength training: Squats (3 sets of 12 reps), lunges (3 sets of 12 reps), push-ups (3 sets of 12 reps)
* Cool-down: 5-minute static stretching (hamstrings, quadriceps, chest)

**Tuesday**

* Warm-up: 5-minute dynamic stretching
* Cardio: 30-minute cycling or swimming
* Strength training: Deadlifts (3 sets of 12 reps), bicep curls (3 sets of 12 reps), tricep dips (3 sets of 12 reps)
* Cool-down: 5-minute static stretching (back, shoulders, biceps)

**Wednesday**

* Rest day

**Thursday**

* Warm-up: 5-minute dynamic stretching
* Cardio: 30-minute high-intensity interval training (HIIT) (sprints, burpees, jump squats)
* Strength training: Chest press (3 sets of 12 reps), rows (3 sets of 12 reps), shoulder press (3 sets of 12 reps)
* Cool-down: 5-minute static stretching (chest, back, shoulders)

**Friday**

* Warm-up: 5-minute dynamic stretching
* Cardio: 30-minute yoga or Pilates
* Strength training: Leg press (3 sets of 12 reps), calf raises (3 sets of 12 reps), core exercises (plank, Russian twists)
* Cool-down: 5-minute static stretching (legs, core, back)

**Saturday**

* Warm-up: 5-minute dynamic stretching
* Cardio: 30-minute outdoor activities (hiking, biking, swimming)
* Strength training: Upper body exercises (push-ups, pull-ups, dumbbell rows)
* Cool-down: 5-minute static stretching (upper body, core, legs)

**Sunday**

* Rest day or active recovery (light yoga, walking, or stretching)

Remember to stay hydrated by drinking plenty of water throughout the day, and to listen to your body and adjust the meal and fitness plan as needed. It's also important to consult with a healthcare professional or registered dietitian before making any significant changes to your diet or exercise routine.

extracted.summary: The response provides a detailed weekly meal and fitness plan, including specific meals and exercises for each day.
